query:
// First, run the SQL-equivalent query to see the "wrong" total
Consumption
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| summarize TotalCost = sum(Consumed * Cost)

// Check for duplicate records - do any records appear more than once?
Consumption
| summarize cnt = count() by Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType, Consumed
| where cnt > 1
| count

// How many duplicates are there and what's the pattern?
Consumption
| summarize cnt = count() by Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType, Consumed
| where cnt > 1
| summarize count() by cnt

// Total records and unique records
Consumption
| summarize TotalRecords=count()
| extend UniqueRecords = TotalRecords - 46591

// Calculate correct total by deduplicating first
Consumption
| distinct Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType, Consumed
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| summarize TotalCost = sum(Consumed * Cost)

// Check if Costs table has duplicates or multiple entries
Costs

query:
// Check unique records per meter type
Consumption
| distinct Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType, Consumed
| summarize UniqueRecords=count() by MeterType

// Check the breakdown by MeterType for both total and deduplicated
Consumption
| summarize TotalConsumed = sum(Consumed) by MeterType
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| extend TotalCost = TotalConsumed * Cost
| project MeterType, TotalConsumed, Cost, TotalCost

// Now with deduplication
Consumption
| distinct Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType, Consumed
| summarize TotalConsumed = sum(Consumed) by MeterType
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| extend TotalCost = TotalConsumed * Cost
| project MeterType, TotalConsumed, Cost, TotalCost

// Check if any household+timestamp+MeterType has multiple different Consumed values
Consumption
| summarize dcount(Consumed), count() by Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType
| where dcount_Consumed > 1
| count

query:
// Check the 3 households with negative values - what's their pattern?

| where HouseholdId in ("DTI532F10619A46A7F8", "DTI03AEB003D8294423", "DTI1AC1DD5E1EEE87C2")
| summarize sum(Consumed) by HouseholdId, MeterType

// Correct approach: for each day/household/meter, get distinct consumed values and sum them
// This handles both exact duplicates (removes them) and negative corrections (cancels them)
Consumption
| summarize Consumed = make_set(Consumed) by Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType
| mv-expand Consumed to typeof(double)
| summarize TotalConsumed = sum(Consumed) by MeterType
| loo

// Try different approach to get distinct values per group
Consumption
| distinct Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType, Consumed
| summarize TotalConsumed = sum(Consumed) by MeterType
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| extend TotalCost = TotalConsumed * Cost
| summarize sum(TotalCost)

// Try: take only one reading per day/household/meter (using arg_max to pick max)
Consumption
| summarize Consumed = arg_max(Consumed, *) by Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType
| summarize TotalConsumed = sum(Consumed) by MeterType
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| extend TotalCost = TotalConsumed * Cost
| summarize sum(TotalCost)

// Let me recompute with high precision using the method from training
// Use the "correct" KQL approach: summarize first, then lookup
Consumption
| summarize TotalConsumed = sum(Consumed) by MeterType
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| extend TotalCost = TotalConsumed*Cost
| project MeterType, TotalConsumed, Cost, TotalCost
| summarize Total = sum(TotalCost)

// Correct approach: first remove exact duplicates using summarize, then compute
// Use summarize to take_any per unique combination
Consumption
| summarize Consumed = take_any(Consumed) by Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType
| summarize TotalConsumed = sum(Consumed) by MeterType
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| extend TotalCost = TotalConsumed * Cost
| project MeterType, Total

// Two fixes: 1) Remove duplicates, 2) Remove negative/zero values
Consumption
| where Consumed > 0
| distinct Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType, Consumed
| summarize TotalConsumed = sum(Consumed) by MeterType
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| extend TotalCost = TotalConsumed * Cost
| project MeterType, TotalConsumed, Cost, TotalCost

// Sum the costs from both meter types
Consumption
| where Consumed > 0
| distinct Timestamp, HouseholdId, MeterType, Consumed
| summarize TotalConsumed = sum(Consumed) by MeterType
| lookup Costs on MeterType
| extend TotalCost = TotalConsumed * Cost
| summarize Total = sum(TotalCost)

// Find cars at the bank (Ave 157, Street 148) during 08:17-08:31
Traffic
| where Ave == 157 and Street == 148
| where Timestamp between (datetime(2022-10-16 08:17) .. datetime(2022-10-16 08:31))
| summarize count() by VIN
| order by count_ desc

// Find cars that were at or very near the bank for extended time during robbery
// Bank at (157, 148). Cars parked there would have multiple readings at the same location
Traffic
| where Ave between (155 .. 159) and Street between (146 .. 150)
| where Timestamp between (datetime(2022-10-16 08:00) .. datetime(2022-10-16 08:35))
| summarize 
    Readings=count(), 
    Min

// How frequently is a typical car recorded?
Traffic
| where VIN == "XCCB8163E6"
| order by Timestamp asc
| take 30

// Find cars that were stationary at/near (157, 148) for at least 2 minutes during 08:00-08:35
// A stationary car would have many readings at the same exact position
Traffic
| where Timestamp between (datetime(2022-10-16 08:00) .. datetime(2022-10-16 08:35))
| where Ave between (155 .. 159) and Street between (146 .. 150)
| summarize 
    Readings=count(),
    MinTime=m

// Check the trajectory of NM47C5A293 (parked 08:09-08:32 near bank)
Traffic
| where VIN == "NM47C5A293"
| order by Timestamp asc
| project Timestamp, Ave, Street

// Look at NM47C5A293 parking spots (gaps > 5 min)
Traffic
| where VIN == "NM47C5A293"
| order by Timestamp asc
| serialize
| extend NextTimestamp = next(Timestamp)
| extend Gap = NextTimestamp - Timestamp
| where Gap > 5m
| project VIN, ParkStart=Timestamp, ParkEnd=NextTimestamp, Gap, Ave, Street

// Find cars that park (have data gaps > 30 min) after 08:31
// Their parked location is where they were last seen before the gap
Traffic
| order by VIN asc, Timestamp asc
| serialize
| extend NextTimestamp = next(Timestamp), NextVIN = next(VIN)
| where VIN == NextVIN
| extend Gap = NextTimestamp - Timestamp
| where Gap > 30m  // parked for at least 30 minutes
| where

query:
// How many distinct users are there?
ChatLogs
| extend User = extract("User '([^']+)'", 1, Message)
| summarize dcount(User)

// Find users who only send DMs and look at their contact count
// First, let's find users who send DMs
ChatLogs
| where Message has "sent message to the user"
| extend Sender = extract("User '([^']+)' sent", 1, Message),
         Receiver = extract("to the user '([^']+)'", 1, Message)
| summarize Contacts = dcount(Receiver), MsgCount = count() by Sender
| where Con

// Look for channels with very few users (exactly 4)
ChatLogs
| where Message has "joined the channel" or Message has "sent message to the channel"
| extend User = extract("User '([^']+)'", 1, Message),
         Channel = extract("channel '([^']+)'", 1, Message)
| summarize UserCount = dcount(User) by Channel
| where UserCount == 4
| order by Channel

query:
// How many distinct workloads start processes?
YaccWorkloadStartsProcess() | summarize dcount(WorkloadId), dcount(Key)

// How deep does process spawning go?
YaccProcessStartsProcess() | summarize dcount(SourceId), dcount(TargetId)

// Check if any process is reachable from multiple apps (would cause double counting)
graph('YaccGraph')
| graph-match (app)-[*1..10]->(proc)
    where labels(app) has "Application"
    and labels(proc) has "Process"
    project AppName = app.AppName, ProcessKey = proc.Key
| summarize Apps = make_set(AppName), AppCount = dcount(AppName) by ProcessKey
| where AppCount > 1

// Check ClassifiedApp details
YaccApplications | where AppName == 'ClassifiedApp'

// Let me also check with max depth to see how many processes are in ClassifiedApp's tree
// and whether there are more at higher depth
graph('YaccGraph')
| graph-match (app)-[*1..10]->(proc)
    where app.AppName == "ClassifiedApp"
    and labels(proc) has "Process"
    project ProcessKey = proc.Key
| summarize count()

// Try with higher depth limit
graph('YaccGraph')
| graph-match (app)-[*1..20]->(proc)
    where labels(app) has "Application"
    and labels(proc) has "Process"
    project ProcessKey = proc.Key
| distinct ProcessKey
| count

let CpuByProcess = YaccProcessEvents
    | where EventType == 'CpuLoad'
    | extend CpuValue = todouble(extract("Value=([0-9.]+)", 1, Message))
    | summarize TotalCpu = sum(CpuValue) by Key = strcat(MachineId, ':', ProcessId);
graph('YaccGraph')
| graph-match (app)-[*1..20]->(proc)
    where labels(app) has "Application"
    and labels(proc) has "Process"
    pro

// What processes does ClassifiedApp run? Look at the process names
graph('YaccGraph')
| graph-match (app)-[*1..20]->(proc)
    where app.AppName == "ClassifiedApp"
    and labels(proc) has "Process"
    project ProcessName = proc.ProcessName
| summarize Count = count() by ProcessName
| top 20 by Count desc
